Orb of the Crimson Bull  
 

No Trade Placeable
Slot: RANGE PRIMARY SECONDARY
Charges: Unlimited
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STA: +10 WIS: +5 INT: +5 HP: +25 MANA: +75
SV FIRE: +10 SV DISEASE: +10 SV COLD: +10 SV MAGIC: +10 SV POISON: +10
Effect: Serpent Sight (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 4.0)
WT: 1.0 Size: TINY
Class: CLR DRU SHM NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Orb of the Crimson Bull
No Trade Placeable
Slot: RANGE PRIMARY SECONDARY
Charges: Unlimited
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STA: +10 WIS: +5 INT: +5 HP: +25 MANA: +75

WT: 1.0 Size: TINY

Race: ALL

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 55

# Dec 18 2003 at 6:24 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592342778640
170 posts
Invigorate is the heal. Invigor is the Sta replenishing spell, which now with the changes to staminia, no longer is worth half a pound of gnoll crap.
▲ Page top
RE: Proc
QuoteReply
# Jul 21 2002 at 11:19 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750607099
121 posts
the stamina restoring one
▲ Page top
hrm
QuoteReply
# Jan 16 2002 at 11:16 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248631031
106 posts
Unless that invigorate proc is like an instant cast it's pretty worthless. There are no negative side effects of having no stamina if yours is above 100. I get the strange feeling that not many lvl 60 tanks in ToV are asking their clerics for a "zing". Besides, shissar and acumen made the "zing" obsolete around oh...lvl 50? Anyway it's a good item with a useless proc.
▲ Page top
RE: hrm
QuoteReply
# Jan 16 2002 at 1:59 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592247673964
Sage
285 posts
Not true at all. Most raids you don't need this but if you go on a raid to under water or airless zone, stamina is very important. If you don't have stamina you just drown faster. When I am on these raids, I am always swapping spells to give zing to others in the group. This would be nice to have so I can don't have to swap spells and still give out zings. Certainly better than what I have on my range slot.
▲ Page top
RE: hrm
QuoteReply
# Aug 23 2002 at 5:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591777964472
51 posts
Yeah but it's VERY situational. If you're gunna use the sta excuse, might as well go for an othmir prexus totem (droppable), at least it has 5 ac and 20 sta. And if you're taking on a mob of this calibre, chances are, you'll have some 56+ shaman with acumen anyways.

Not Lore
QuoteReply
# Sep 22 2001 at 12:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765258974
183 posts
You could have one in second range and primary
▲ Page top
jesus
QuoteReply
# Jul 26 2001 at 8:26 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591764036108
33 posts
I only speak to the people who dont know what they are talking about and are dr00ling over this item. The others who are 55+ who go to this zone, you can skip this post. Since when is an epic item a piece of **** to some of you? Its one of the best things any of you will ever have. I am a wizard who is fortunate enough to have his epic and also fortunate that my epic isnt as hard as most of the others. In fact, it was pretty easy. I got lucky with Phinigal, only took me once to kill for the staff, was in plane of fear and only took me twice to get the golem and VS dropped the staff as usual so i was able to complete my epic pretty damn fast. The barrier of shield is an 800 hp absorbant shield that shields me from AEs in ToV and the **** that melees for over 200 alot, gives the tanks time to taunt of me in case i accidently overnuke or something. It has great resists and the +25 int allows me to put on a ******** of resist gear instead of the other crap that i had before. Before you start mocking epics, do your research and get your own class epic. I havent seen one damn thing i would trade my epic for.

Another thing is, unless the info is incorrect on this, its PRIMARY, SECONDARY AND RANGE. I dont have brain of cazic thule yet so i would gladl stick this in that slot until i do and then once i finally have CTs brain, i will put this item into my secondary slot. Look at this f*cking item. The resists are great and since most of you have never been to ToV, a caster without resists will die pretty damn f*cking fast. I resists of the **** that they cast on me now because of my gear and whatever i dont resist, my epic shield absorbs for me. So before any of you start comparing the ******* epics to ToV, sleepers items, look them up, dont be stupid.

thats my 2cp
▲ Page top
Simple~
QuoteReply

No ac, Good for resist, but it's int caster all the way, cause if your a wis caster and your going to ToV then u should have a sarnak battle shield or u will become roast/frozen <Insert race here> =P

#Anonymous, Posted: May 17 2001 at 6:55 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Only slightly better than Mirenilla's other awful drops, this doesn't even beat out an epic. You might be able to toss it to some poor wizard who never completed his epic, though.
RE: A piece of crap...NOT!
QuoteReply
# Jul 23 2001 at 3:42 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762161646
19 posts
Actually, I'd be glad to take it even if I had my epic. Note that the wizard epic is a 1HB. That leaves the other hand free. Now, what do you want for your other hand that has better stats than this?

Fact is, nothing that goes in the secondary slot has more mana than this, and the resists aren't bad either.

QuoteReply
# May 15 2001 at 9:52 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Why would a priest class want to use this? The only thing it has going for it is the saves, but you can't hit with it and it has no AC. Enchanters wouldn't want it either, cause it has no AC and no CHA. So, that leaves the other pure caster classes. Might be nice for them. The effect is kinda weak tho.
▲ Page top
RE: <sigh>
QuoteReply
# Mar 22 2002 at 5:25 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249641124
79 posts
your absolutly right who besides you, would actually want 10 extra STA, 5 extra int/wis, plus 10 to every save, 75 extra mana and 25 extra hp
but by the time you get this wis/int is going to be 200+ (most the time) so hell all its got going for it is: saves, sta, hp, & mana

your right i dont want this anymore
▲ Page top
RE: <sigh>
QuoteReply
# Jan 16 2002 at 10:23 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
So you can't hit with it. No wis caster other than a shammy is going to try to melee much over level 25-30 anyway. My cleric's stuns do more damage than he can dish out melee. Wisdom, mana and +10 to all saves is very nice. Of course, a cleric in this raid probably has his epic, and would not put this in primary. Also would be likely to have a good shield, so not in secondary. But would make a great range item. Only a few better ones out there, and they are very rare. A wis caster could easily have a far inferior item in range, even at the level necessary to do this raid.

Not saying it is not wonderful for int caster, just that a wis caster is likely to want one as well.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 15 2001 at 7:53 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) this is a chanter item all the way i dont care what anyone says. a chanter using epic staff and this would be fookin leet. nice upgrade to bio orb but must be hard as hell to get
#Anonymous, Posted: May 15 2001 at 8:20 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I assume you just said that to be an *** because there is 0 reason for this to be an enchanter item before anyone else. Ususally some moron will say its an enchanter item because it has +1 CHA or something, this has 0 CHA. So either you are prentending to be an idiot or you are an idiot which is it?
RE: chanter item
QuoteReply
# May 15 2001 at 10:01 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591757690976
36 posts
It's an enchanter item about as much as it's a nec/wiz/mag item. I think this is probably more valuable to a pure caster then a priest, plus priests get some pretty nice shields with similar stats that are much easier to get then this. There is a lot of "this would be great for.." posts on this board, it's mainly 10 year olds hoping that their post will mean they will get this before anyone else. I play a 55 chanter and cha and ac don't mean a whole lot to me, HP/sta, saves, and mana are all I worry about. The effect is good to you dont have to mem the spell to cast on your tanks, but it would've been better as extinguish fatigue. And the effect is really weak if it's self only, since all the classes rarely melee after level 30. It's a decent well rounded item.
▲ Page top
RE: chanter item
